reviewer: new-demo build wave (in-browser runnability proof first, then headless-Chrome real download+inference on index + multi-model chain, all-ladder mobile+desktop overflow matrix, cached auto-init behaviour)

rubric.evidence: REAL in-browser inference verified BEFORE building: an ORT-web WASM harness (onnxruntime-web@1.21.0, dynamic import) on inference4j/craft-mlt-25k (model.onnx, ~83 MB) — session inputs=['input'], outputs=['score_map','feature_map']; score_map dims [1,272,368,2] (region ch0 + affinity ch1). On the cafe sign the REGION map max 1.011 / mean 0.062 / 2.22% of pixels >0.7 and the AFFINITY map max 0.977 / mean 0.023 / 0.74% >0.7 — the classic detection signature: low background, localised high-score blobs ON the actual text. transformers.js@3.7.5 has NO CRAFT class and no text-detection pipeline task, so the demo runs the ONNX graph directly via raw ONNX Runtime Web in a Web Worker (isolated per-worker pin, like scene-text-detection/face-embedding). Headless build verify: fresh profile -> Download (~83 MB) -> ready -> Detect produced 20 word boxes on the cafe sign, 0 console errors, 0 net failures.
rubric.notes: Model inference4j/craft-mlt-25k (MIT), NCHW [1,3,H,W] ImageNet-normalised input -> score_map [1,H/2,W/2,2]; the CRAFT decode (region>textThreshold OR affinity>linkThreshold -> connected components -> min-area oriented box -> unclip) is in the worker.

rubric.evidence: Output shape + semantic sanity confirmed on every page (boxes drawn on the image, counts match content): index/basics found 20 word boxes on the cafe sign; the multi-model chain detected 20 words and TrOCR read the top 8 to real fragments of the sign (THE / CORNER / CAFE / COFFEE & / PASTRIES / FRESH / OPEN 7). The two raw heatmaps (region + affinity) are shown side by side in 'See inside', with a per-region confidence + angle + size list.
rubric.notes: object-detection task template ('bounding boxes with class labels + confidence drawn on the image') fits: here the single class is 'word region', with per-region confidence + recovered angle. CRAFT word boxes are tighter than TrOCR's line training, so some multi-model reads are word fragments — honest, real recognition of the actual pixels, never faked.

rubric.evidence: conformance declares-model-id=pass (inference4j/craft-mlt-25k linked to its card), declares-task=pass (object-detection), declares-backend=pass (WASM), declares-quantisation=pass (fp32), inference-off-main-thread=pass (new Worker + worker.js). At-a-glance names the exact model, runtime (onnxruntime-web 1.21), NCHW input / region+affinity output shapes, and honestly notes transformers.js has no CRAFT class — no invented API.

conformance shared-auto-init-loader=pass — every page loads through createModelLoader. The worker fetches the ONNX THROUGH Cache Storage (craft-mlt-onnx-cache) with a content-length Response so lib/model-cache.js scans '/inference4j/craft-mlt-25k/' and the shared loader auto-inits on a returning visit (no Download button), plus the per-model clear-cache control works. First visit shows the honest Download (~83 MB) state and never auto-downloads.

conformance loads-clean=pass (0 console errors on load across index + 4 ladder pages, verified headless), live-status-region=pass (role=status aria-live), errors-on-the-page=pass. Download progress streams from the worker (content-length) into the loader progress bar; failures surface in the .status.err line and the loader offers Retry. Fresh-profile load shows the honest Download state.
rubric.notes: runs-without-webgpu-fallback: WASM-only (requiresWebGPU:false) — runs everywhere, verified on the no-GPU headless runner.

rubric.evidence: Every control exercised headless and is labelled + keyboard-operable: sample thumbs (role=button, tabindex, Enter/Space), dropzones + file inputs, Detect / Redact / Download-redacted / Detect&read buttons, and the character-core / character-link / detection-resolution <input type=range> sliders. buttons-have-names=pass, images-have-alt=pass. The practical Download-redacted button exports the in-page canvas — no upload.
rubric.notes: The textThreshold + linkThreshold sliders re-run detection with new CRAFT params (a deliberate, bounded single forward pass off-thread).

rubric.evidence: All boxes, both heatmaps, and the counts come from the on-device ORT-web run — no canned values. Distinct, correct results per image and real TrOCR reads of the detected crops prove the output tracks the real region+affinity maps, not a placeholder.

conformance images-have-alt=pass, buttons-have-names=pass, live-status-region=pass. Sample images have descriptive alt; the detection canvas + both heatmap canvases carry role=img + aria-label; the decoded-region list is a real <ol> so a screen reader gets the count + per-region confidence/angle as text (the accessible fallback for canvas content per the expose-canvas-content guide). Status uses role=status aria-live=polite.
rubric.notes: Sample images are announced as generated-for-this-demo (no real people/brands).

rubric.evidence: conformance responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass. Measured mobile (360x740 DPR3) horizontal overflow = 0 px on index + all 4 ladder pages, and desktop (1280x800) = 0 px, 0 console errors on each. The two-column (image | controls) + heat-pair (region | affinity) layouts use flex:1 1 <basis> + min-inline-size:0 and stack on mobile; canvases are inline-size:100% block-size:auto; no clipped controls. Dark + light both legible.
rubric.notes: aspect-ratio-free canvases sized from the image; no 100vh traps.

rubric.evidence: Inference AND the full CRAFT decode (threshold region+affinity, connected-components labeling, convex hull + rotating-calipers min-area rect, unclip) run in a Web Worker; both heatmaps are transferred back as Uint8 buffers (zero-copy) and the main thread only draws boxes/heatmaps. No main-thread long task during interaction — the dense-output composite hotspot is avoided by keeping the pixel work in the worker.
rubric.notes: Guidance applied: break-up-long-tasks / identify-inp-causes (inference + dense pixel decode off the main thread; transfer not clone).

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive image + controls two column and side-by-side heatmaps no horizontal overflow on mobile flexbox
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flexbox with flex:1 1 <basis> and set min-inline-size:0 on flex items with wide content so they shrink instead of overflowing; use gap + flex-wrap; size media with inline-size:100% block-size:auto.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — the image/controls two-column and the region|affinity heat-pair use flex:1 1 <basis> with min-inline-size:0 and stack on narrow viewports; canvases are inline-size:100% block-size:auto. Verified 0 px horizontal overflow at 360x740 AND 1280x800 on all 5 pages.

guidanceConsulted.query: make canvas bounding-box detection output accessible to screen readers
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Regular canvas content is invisible to assistive tech; provide an accessible representation (role=img + aria-label, and a real text list of the content).
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied (fallback strategy) — HTML-in-canvas (requestPaint) is not Baseline, so instead each detection/heatmap canvas has role=img + a count-bearing aria-label, and the decoded regions are rendered as a real <ol> list (confidence/angle/size per region) that screen readers and find-in-page can reach.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move image inference and pixel post-processing off main thread web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move long main-thread JavaScript (inference + dense pixel loops) off the main thread into a worker; transfer buffers instead of cloning.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — ONNX inference and the entire CRAFT connected-components + min-area-rect decode run in a module worker; the region + affinity heatmaps are transferred back as Uint8 buffers, and the main thread only draws. No blocking loop during interaction.

openQuestions: CRAFT gives word-level boxes; a follow-up could add a line-merge heuristic (group horizontally-adjacent words) before TrOCR so multi-word lines read as one line.
A deskew step using each word's recovered angle before TrOCR would straighten rotated crops and improve the reads.

followUpGoals.goal: Add a live-camera ladder demo (lib/capture-ux.js, user-initiated) that highlights word regions in a webcam frame for assistive capture.
Add a CRAFT-vs-DBNet side-by-side comparison page (this model vs scene-text-detection) so visitors can see how the two detection philosophies differ on the same image.

summary: New demo: on-device CRAFT text DETECTION (locate words, not read) via inference4j/craft-mlt-25k (MIT) run through raw ONNX Runtime Web in a worker, with the full CRAFT decode (region + affinity thresholds -> connected components -> min-area oriented word box -> unclip) off the main thread. A distinct architecture from the built DBNet scene-text detector: CRAFT reasons about characters and the affinity links between them, exposed as two heatmaps in 'See inside'. Index + 4 ladder pages (basics/practical/wild/multi-model incl. CRAFT->TrOCR), all verified with real in-browser inference, cached auto-init, clean console, and mobile+desktop parity (0px overflow both classes). Sample images are generated for this demo (no real people/brands).
